title: Kensa Native Evidence (v1)
description: Schema for Kensa's native evidence document, emitted by `kensa check`/`remediate` via `-o evidence:`. Full-file parity with the Python Kensa evidence format: session + host context (capabilities, effective variables) + per-rule results carrying structured per-check observation evidence + a summary. The per-check evidence is the reproducible proof (command, output, expected vs actual) that lets an auditor verify a finding without re-running the scan (KensaLabs evidence-engineering principle).

version.description: Evidence document schema version (semver). v1 documents set the major to 1.

rules_path.description: Rules directory or source used for the scan; may be empty.

command.description: The kensa subcommand that produced this document.

hostname.description: Target host as kensa addressed it (SSH host/address).

family.description: OS family ID from /etc/os-release (e.g. rhel, ubuntu).

version.description: OS major version (int) or full VERSION_ID (string).

capabilities.description: Detected capability set (probe name -> bool). Open map: new probes may appear without a schema change.

effective_variables.description: The resolved rule-variable set after the full tier merge (embedded defaults -> config-dir -> per-group -> per-host -> CLI). Values are the strings/numbers substituted into templated rules. Open map.

results.description: One entry per rule evaluated, in scan order.

fixed.description: Remediate path only: rules brought into compliance this run.

passed.description: True when the check confirmed desired state.

skipped.description: True when the rule did not apply to the host (platform/no-impl) and was not evaluated.

errored.description: True when the check could not run; mutually exclusive with a clean pass/fail.

skip_reason.description: Why the rule was skipped (populated when skipped is true).

remediated.description: Remediate path only: a fix was applied this run.

evidence.description: Structured observation evidence, one entry per check the rule ran (a single-check rule yields a one-element array; a `checks:` list yields one per sub-check). May be empty when no check ran (e.g. a skipped rule).

frameworks.description: Framework -> native control identifier the rule maps to (e.g. {"nist_800_53":"CM-7","cis_rhel9_v2":"3.2.1"}). Open map.

checkEvidence.description: The reproducible proof for one check: the exact command run and its observed result versus the expected value.

method.description: The check method (e.g. kernel_module_state, sysctl_value, config_value).

command.description: The exact command executed on the host.

stdout.description: Captured standard output (may be truncated; see `truncated`).

stderr.description: Captured standard error (may be truncated; see `truncated`).

actual.description: The value/state observed on the host.

truncated.description: True when `stdout`/`stderr` were truncated at the per-field byte cap (64 KiB). The retained content is the leading bytes.
